nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=128 beats='missing the last tram leaves Priya shut in overnight in the Everbloom Conservatory, a wonderland of brass and tin and copper creatures that have all wound down and gone perfectly still; her firefly Wisp rides on her shoulder, ticking softly as if to say come on, come on; four great wings open off the glass entry hall, the fern aviary, the meadow terrace, the lily pond hall, and the orchid dome, and Priya must pick one to explore before her lantern oil running low'>>

choices.label: Slip into the lily pond hall.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Priya steps into the fern aviary where hundreds of clockwork birds sit frozen on their perches, beaks open in songs that stopped hours ago, and her firefly Wisp ticks softly on her shoulder as if urging her to hurry'>>

choices.label: Walk in under the silent birds.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the aviary splits two ways, a wide floor of tall perch-trees leading deeper among the biggest birds, and a narrow spiral stair climbing to the nesting ledge near the dark glass roof'>>

choices.label: Climb the spiral stair to the nesting ledge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='among the perch-trees Priya can turn toward the hummingbird nook, or toward the nesting ledge where a huge brass eagle spreads its still wings'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the hummingbird nook is a wall of tiny brass larks and wrens, each no bigger than a thumb, their little keys wound down to nothing'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='her firefly Wisp scampers to a dusty seed-jar on a shelf, and Priya sees it once played all the birds at once, if only it had power'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='behind a dusty seed-jar on a shelf a small painted tile is pinned, and it speaks of the gardener's dawn key that wakes the whole museum from the fountain court'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Priya pockets the clue and follows her firefly Wisp along a mossy garden walk that runs behind the cases toward the middle of the building'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='a mossy garden walk ends at a low wooden gate with a sundial mark, the heart of the museum humming faintly beyond'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the nesting ledge is all shadows and great still wings, a brass eagle and a bronze owl watching with glassy eyes that do not blink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='a single long feather has fallen, a single fallen twig shaped like a key, but when Priya lifts it she finds it is only a pretty decoy, not the real thing'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the bronze owl, though, has one wing pointing stiffly toward the statue's pointing wing, as if a keeper set it that way long ago on purpose'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Priya follows where the owl points, down a narrow garden stair toward the warm hum at the center of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways bring Priya to the fern archway that opens on the fountain court, and her firefly Wisp squeaks and darts ahead through the gap'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the door swings on a round hall lit by a high skylight of the sky warming toward golden sunrise, the true middle of the whole clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='in the center of the rotunda stands the great sundial, and Priya is sure the gardener's dawn key must hang near it'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the nesting ledge's spiral stair is rickety, and halfway up a wobbly plank that creaks underfoot gives a warning crack beneath her shoe'>>

choices.label: Grab the rail and climb back down.
Climb on toward the high nesting ledge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Priya freezes, grabs the rail with both hands, and waits until the shaking stops, her heart pounding, then backs carefully down to the safe aviary floor'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='safe on the floor again, she lets out a long breath, decides the high nesting ledge is not worth the danger, and turns instead to the perch-trees'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='Priya climbs on despite the groaning, but a wobbly plank that creaks underfoot folds and the stair pulls away from the wall, leaving her stuck on a narrow ledge among the sleeping birds; she is quite safe, but she can only wait up there, waving to her firefly Wisp below, until the museum staff arrive at opening time and fetch a ladder to bring her down'>>

ending.title: Stranded in the high nesting ledge

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the meadow terrace opens wide and golden, a whole plain of tin grass with the stone garden lion caught mid-roar, giraffes as tall as the ceiling, and a great elephant standing dark and heavy in the middle distance'>>

choices.label: Step out onto the tin plain.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Priya can cross the open savanna floor toward the big animals, or slip along the painted horizon wall where a narrow walkway skirts the edge of the hall'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='out on the floor Priya can head for the deer herd by the pond, or the willow grove where the tall necks reach up into shadow'>>

choices.label: Approach the deer herd by the pond.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='up close the great tin elephant of the deer herd by the pond is enormous, and as Priya passes, a last bit of wind makes its trunk twitch and one foot scrape, and she jumps back with a gasp'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the elephant lurches a slow half-step, groaning, not truly awake but not truly asleep, and Priya sees she must give it a wide and careful berth'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she ducks behind a broad oak tree and waits until the great beast settles again with a long mechanical sigh, still at last'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='safe past the elephant, Priya finds a worn garden trail marked with petals in the tin grass, leading toward the heart of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='a worn garden trail marked with petals ends at a plain gate in the meadow wall, warm to the touch, with the hum of the great sundial behind it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the willow grove is a forest of long tin necks and gentle glass eyes, their heads lost up in the roof shadows'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='high on a shelf, almost out of reach, Priya spies a basket raised on a rope pulley, and a rope pulley the keeper used to raise and lower it'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she works the pulley and lowers the shelf, but a basket raised on a rope pulley holds only spare oil-cans, not keys, though a painted signpost points the way onward'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='following a painted signpost, Priya crosses the grove to a garden door humming with the warmth of the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both routes meet at the meadow gate that opens on the fountain court, and her firefly Wisp slips under it with a flick of its tail'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='beyond the arch the round rotunda waits under its the sky warming toward golden sunrise, the true middle of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the great sundial towers in the center, and Priya feels sure the gardener's dawn key must hang somewhere near it'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the horizon-wall walkway is narrow, and as Priya edges along it the drowsy garden deer blocking the way lurches awake below and swings its heavy trunk, groaning, right across the way ahead'>>

choices.label: Press into an alcove and wait.
Try to hurry past the drowsy garden deer blocking the way.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Priya presses flat into a shallow alcove in the wall and holds still, letting the drowsy garden deer blocking the way sway and settle until it goes quiet, then she slips back to the open floor'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='back on the savanna floor with the elephant still again, Priya decides the wall walkway is too risky and picks a path among the big animals instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='Priya tries to hurry past on the narrow walkway, but the drowsy garden deer blocking the way swings round and gently, blindly, backs her into the corner of the hall where she cannot get by; there is no harm in it, only a big slow tin beast in the way, and she must wait tucked safely in the corner until morning, when the keepers wind the elephant down and free her'>>

ending.title: Cornered by the drowsy garden deer blocking the way

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the lily pond hall is cool and blue, great copper whales hanging on wires overhead, a huge octopus coiled in a corner, and glass tanks of still copper fish glinting in the dim light'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Priya can wade between the low floor tanks toward the middle of the hall, or climb the whale-rib catwalk that arches high over everything in the dark'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='among the tanks Priya can explore the lily cabinet, or the deep pond diorama where the water-glass turns almost black'>>

choices.label: Peer into the deep pond diorama.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the lily cabinet glitters with copper seahorses and darting fish, all frozen, and a brass tap that hums at a touch hums when Priya rests her hand there'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='her firefly Wisp taps a brass tap that hums at a touch and a panel slides back to show a folded map of the whole conservatory, every hall drawn in careful ink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='on a folded map of the whole conservatory a small tag shaped like a key is tied, and a note in neat writing points to the fountain court at the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Priya tucks a folded map of the whole conservatory away and follows its inked corridors, her firefly Wisp leading, toward the warm middle of the building'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the way ends at a round glass door like a porthole, humming softly, the great sundial ticking somewhere close beyond it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the deep pond diorama is the darkest place Priya has been, black glass all around, and only a faint glow far down in it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the glow comes from a firefly lamp with a little glow left, casting just enough light to show a low hatch set in the pond wall'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='a firefly lamp with a little glow left flickers, and by it Priya reads a sign painted with an arrow above a low hatch set in the pond wall'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she opens a low hatch set in the pond wall and follows a snug garden passage leading inward, warm and humming, toward the center of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways bring Priya to the pond archway that opens on the fountain court, and her firefly Wisp squeezes eagerly through'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='past the door the great rotunda opens under its the sky warming toward golden sunrise, the true heart of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the great sundial stands at its center, and Priya is certain the gardener's dawn key must be near it'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the whale-rib catwalk sways as Priya climbs it, and out over the middle of the dark hall a loose lily-pad bridge over the gap swings loose with a clang, leaving a gap she would have to jump'>>

choices.label: Cling to the rope and back off.
Try to jump a loose lily-pad bridge over the gap in the dark.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Priya backs off a loose lily-pad bridge over the gap, clinging to the guide rope, and edges her way carefully back to the solid start of the catwalk, her hands aching'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='safe at the foot of the catwalk again, Priya decides the high dark crossing is not worth it and wades in among the floor tanks instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='Priya tries to jump the gap in the dark, but she cannot see the far side and freezes on a loose lily-pad bridge over the gap, too scared to go on or back; she clings there safely above the soft tank-nets until, at opening time, a keeper hears her firefly Wisp squeaking and helps her down, her night of exploring over'>>

ending.title: Lost in the dark pond hall

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the orchid dome glows softly, clockwork butterflies and beetles resting on glass ferns beneath a domed ceiling painted with stars, everything still and glittering'>>

choices.label: Wander in among the glass ferns.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Priya can wander through the low fern beds among the resting insects, or climb the iron mezzanine gallery that runs around the glass walls above'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='in the fern beds Priya can visit the moth vault, or the ladybird corner where little brass beetles crowd a keeper bench'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the moth vault is full of clockwork butterflies with wings of thin bright foil, folded and still, waiting for a wind that never came'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='her firefly Wisp noses a tiny winder left on a stand, and Priya sees each butterfly has a keyhole no bigger than a seed, far too small for any ordinary key'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='beside a tiny winder left on a stand a card telling of one dawn key tells of one the gardener's dawn key that turns the whole museum at once from the fountain court, and Priya memorises the way'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she follows the mossy path out of the vault, her firefly Wisp scurrying ahead between the glass ferns toward the middle of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the mossy path out of the vault ends at a mirrored door edged with vines, humming gently, the great sundial beating slow and deep beyond it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the ladybird corner is a busy little world of brass beetles gathered round a keeper bench scattered with tiny tools'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='in a half-open drawer Priya finds a folded map in a half-open drawer of the conservatory and the halls beyond, marked in a keeper hand'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='a folded map in a half-open drawer shows a short way to the fountain court, and a drawn ladybird pointing its feelers seems to point along the route'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Priya follows the mapped way between the glass beds between the glass cases to a mirrored door edged with vines, warm and humming, that leads toward the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways lead Priya to the orchid archway that opens on the fountain court, and her firefly Wisp slips through the crack ahead of her'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the arch gives on the round rotunda under its the sky warming toward golden sunrise, the true center of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the great sundial rises in the middle, and Priya knows the gardener's dawn key must hang close by'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the mezzanine gallery is edged with old glass panels, and as Priya leans to look down, a glass pane shifting loose on the rail shifts loose with a grinding sound and tips slowly inward'>>

choices.label: Step back from the loose glass.
Squeeze past a glass pane shifting loose on the rail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Priya snatches her hands back and steps away as a glass pane shifting loose on the rail settles crookedly against the rail, then she retreats along the gallery to the safe stairs down'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='back on the conservatory floor, shaken, Priya decides the mezzanine is too fragile and explores the fern beds below instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='Priya edges past a glass pane shifting loose on the rail, but another shifts behind her and a glass door swings shut across the gallery, closing her gently in the glass dome; nothing breaks and she is quite safe, but she must wait, tapping to her firefly Wisp through the glass, until the keepers open the gallery in the morning'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='BOTTLENECK: every wing of the museum leads here to the fountain court, and Priya finds what she came for; high on a brass hook beside the great sundial hangs the gardener's dawn key, the one key that can wind the whole menagerie at once; she climbs a little garden ladder and lifts it down, warm and heavy, while her firefly Wisp cheers in tiny squeaks; three ways lead on from the rotunda, through the great root-clock doors, along the gallery of old gardener portraits, or down the narrow back stairs'>>

choices.label: Push through the great root-clock doors.
Follow the gallery of old gardener portraits.
Slip down the narrow back stairs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the great root-clock doors open on the vast root-clock room where the whole museum clockwork lives, wheels and shafts reaching up into the dark'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya follows a walk between the still roots, her firefly Wisp pattering ahead, toward a great silent seed-drum at the very center of it all'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='a walk between the still roots ends above the deep works of the entire conservatory, waiting cold and quiet'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the gallery of old gardener portraits is lined with paintings of old keepers, and their painted eyes seem to follow Priya toward a door at the far end'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the last portrait is the last portrait, a kind old gardener, and beneath it a brass plate reads old Marigold, tender of the works'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the door below his portrait opens on a walkway that looks down into the vast root-clock room, the heart of the whole museum'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the narrow back stairs are cramped and dark, and at the bottom a garden turnstile, its lock stiff with age bars the way; Priya can try to squeeze carefully through the gap beside it, or shove hard against the turnstile itself'>>

choices.label: Shove hard against a garden turnstile, its lock stiff with age.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya breathes in and slips sideways through the narrow gap, her firefly Wisp riding her collar, into a low passage beyond the turnstile beyond a garden turnstile, its lock stiff with age'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='a low passage beyond the turnstile climbs and opens onto a ledge above the vast root-clock room, the deep works of the entire conservatory spread out below'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='Priya shoves a garden turnstile, its lock stiff with age with all her weight, but it swings the wrong way and latches shut behind her, penning her in the little dark stairwell with no way back up; she is safe and her firefly Wisp keeps her company, but she can only sit and wait on the steps until the morning staff come and unlatch a garden turnstile, its lock stiff with age to let her out'>>

ending.title: Turned Around at a garden turnstile, its lock stiff with age

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='BOTTLENECK: all three ways bring Priya out above the root-clock chamber, and here she learns why the whole menagerie fell asleep; the great root-clock has snapped clean across, its broken ends sprung apart, and nothing can be wound until it is mended; even the gardener's dawn key is useless against a broken heart of steel; her firefly Wisp scratches worriedly at the floor, and Priya sees three ways to look for help, the gardener's toolbox, the wall of planting manuals, and the scratched patch of soil'>>

choices.label: Search the wall of planting manuals.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the gardener's toolbox is a deep tin chest of spanners and oil, and on top lies a heavy, unfamiliar trowel'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='her firefly Wisp burrows under the tools and turns up a card of gardener repair instructions, a keeper drawing of how a broken spring is clamped and changed'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='a card of gardener repair instructions in hand, Priya follows a keeper sign from the gardener's toolbox toward a low door half-hidden behind the roots'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the wall of planting manuals is stacked with worn keeper books, their spines labelled winding, oiling, and mending the great spring'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya pulls down the mending book and its pages fall open to the planting book's picture of a hidden shed'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the book names a low door half-hidden behind the roots, and Priya hurries to find it, her firefly Wisp scampering at her heels'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='where her firefly Wisp scratches, a loose floor panel hides a crawlspace, but a heavy, half-braced root over the panel leans over it, only half-braced; Priya can ease the panel up slowly and gently, or yank it free in a hurry'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya lifts the panel a careful inch at a time, keeping clear of a heavy, half-braced root over the panel, and uncovers a low crawlspace behind the root-clock running behind the machine'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='she crawls through a low crawlspace behind the root-clock with her firefly Wisp and comes up beside a low door half-hidden behind the roots, the very one the keeper must have used'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='Priya yanks the panel free, but the sudden jerk unbalances a heavy, half-braced root over the panel and it topples with a great clang across a low crawlspace behind the root-clock, blocking it and pinning the hem of her coat; she wriggles safely out of her coat, unhurt, but the way is buried now and she must wait by the great root-clock until the keepers arrive at dawn to shift the gear'>>

ending.title: Pinned by a heavy, half-braced root over the panel

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='BOTTLENECK: each way leads to the same low door, and behind it Priya finds the gardener's hidden potting shed, snug and warm and full of wonders; here are spare springs coiled on pegs, oil and clamps and the special tool for changing a mainspring, and on the bench old Marigold's note explaining that he has been taken poorly and cannot come, with careful drawings of how to mend the great spring; now Priya has the gardener's dawn key, a fresh coiled vine-spring, and the knowledge to use them; three things call to her, the open planting manual, the vine-spring's hook, and old Marigold's note'>>

choices.label: Take down a fresh coiled vine-spring from the vine-spring's hook.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the open planting manual lies open at the very page Priya needs, each step of changing the great spring drawn in patient keeper ink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya reads it twice until she is sure she understands, her firefly Wisp following her finger down the page like a tiny student'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='confident now, she gathers the tool and a fresh coiled vine-spring and heads back toward the root-clock chamber to begin'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='a fresh coiled vine-spring hangs coiled and gleaming on the vine-spring's hook, as long as Priya is tall, heavier than it looks when she lifts it down'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='she loads it onto the little garden cart with the changing tool, her firefly Wisp perched on top to keep watch'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='wheeling the little garden cart carefully, Priya makes her way back toward the broken heart of the museum'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='old Marigold's note mentions a rusted shortcut lever for the cart that could speed the heavy spring back to the root-clock chamber; Priya can carry the spring the long safe way in her own arms, or throw a rusted shortcut lever for the cart to send the little garden cart quickly'>>

choices.label: Carry the spring the long safe way.
Throw a rusted shortcut lever for the cart.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya lifts the heavy spring in her own arms and carries it step by careful step, her firefly Wisp cheering her on, back toward the root-clock chamber'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='breathing hard, she sets the spring down beside the broken one, everything she needs now gathered in one place'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='Priya throws a rusted shortcut lever for the cart and the little garden cart jerks away too fast, rattling off down a side track with a fresh coiled vine-spring aboard and vanishing behind a locked shutter; nothing is broken and Priya is fine, but a fresh coiled vine-spring is out of reach now, and she can only wait by the machine until the keepers open the shutter in the morning'>>

ending.title: The Runaway the little garden cart

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='BOTTLENECK: every path ends here at the root-clock chamber, where Priya stands with the gardener's dawn key in one hand, a shining a fresh coiled vine-spring at her feet, and old Marigold's instructions clear in her head; through the tall windows the sky warming toward golden sunrise, and soon the doors will open and children will pour in hoping to see the animals move; there is time for one brave choice; Priya can try to change the great spring and rewind the whole menagerie, mend just one beloved creature she has come to love, follow old Marigold's deepest secret, or simply keep the museum safe and let the quiet dawn come'>>

choices.label: Change the great spring and rewind them all.
Keep the museum safe until dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='changing the great root-clock is the hardest task of all, and how Priya does it matters; she can work slow and careful by the manual, work bold and fast to beat the dawn, or try a clever trick from old Marigold's notes to make it easier'>>

choices.label: Work slow and careful by the manual.
Work bold and fast to beat the dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='step by careful step Priya clamps the great root-clock, eases the ends apart, and fits a fresh coiled vine-spring exactly as the manual shows, her firefly Wisp passing her each tool'>>

choices.label: Fit the master key to the shaft.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the new spring is in and the gardener's dawn key fits the great winding shaft; Priya can wind it fully now so the whole menagerie wakes for the morning crowd, or wind it gently and set it to stir only when old Marigold himself returns'>>

choices.label: Wind it fully and wake them all for dawn.
Set them to wake when Fettle returns.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='racing the light, Priya forces the spring in fast; it is nearly seated but not quite, and she must choose, to stop and reseat it properly or wind it now and hope'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya makes herself slow down, backs the spring out, and seats it properly this time, and the great works take the gardener's dawn key with a deep satisfying click'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='following old Marigold's clever notes, Priya links a fresh coiled vine-spring through a helper gear that takes half the strain, and the mend goes smoother than she dared hope'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='with the trick done, the great root-clock holds firm, and the gardener's dawn key turns the whole museum with a low waking hum'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='Priya winds the gardener's dawn key and all through the museum the menagerie stirs to life at once, brass birds bursting into song, the stone garden lion stretching, the great carved pond carp turning slow on its wires, the great elephant lifting its trunk in a gentle trumpet; when the doors open at dawn the children gasp with joy to see every animal moving, and old Marigold, hearing the news, weeps with gladness that a stranger saved his whole life's work; the very best of endings'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='Priya sets the great root-clock gently, so the animals will wake slow and easy the moment old Marigold turns the gardener's dawn key himself; she leaves everything mended and ready, and when old Marigold comes home at last he finds his menagerie whole and waiting, and knows a kind hand has been here; a warm and thoughtful ending'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='the reseated spring holds, and Priya winds the museum awake; most of the menagerie stirs beautifully, and though a few small far-off animals sleep on, the morning crowd still marvels at a hall of moving wonders, and Priya grins to have very nearly done it all; a proud ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='Priya winds the half-seated spring and it slips with a twang, springing loose so the mend must be done all over again; nothing is harmed, but there is no time now before the doors open, and the animals sleep on through the morning while Priya waits, a little sad, to explain it all to the keepers; a gentle setback'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='Priya's clever mend holds strong, and the gardener's dawn key wakes the menagerie smoothly, animal after animal coming alive down the halls; the keepers later shake their heads in wonder at the neat helper gear, and say the child who did this has the makings of a fine keeper; a clever, happy ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Priya cannot mend the whole great spring in time, but with the gardener's dawn key and a little spare wind she could wake just one animal, and she thinks of the ones she has met tonight; the garden hummingbird, the stone garden lion, the great carved pond carp, a painted garden moth, or the once-drowsy garden deer'>>

choices.label: Wake the great carved pond carp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya hurries to the fern aviary and fits the little winder to the garden hummingbird's tiny keyhole, turning it with the greatest care'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the garden hummingbird trembles, ruffles its brass feathers, and her firefly Wisp watches in delight as the little bird gathers itself to sing'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the garden hummingbird bursts into the sweetest clockwork song, filling the whole aviary with silver notes, and when the doors open the children crowd to hear the one bird singing its heart out in the hush; old Marigold says a museum with even one waking animal is a museum full of hope; a bright, joyful ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya crosses to the meadow terrace and fits the key to the stone garden lion, winding until she feels its spring grow tight and ready'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the stone garden lion shivers, its tin mane shaking, and slowly lifts its heavy head as the last of the wind takes hold'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the stone garden lion opens its jaws and gives a long rolling roar that echoes through every hall, and the sleeping museum seems to shiver with it; the morning children jump and then cheer, and Priya laughs, proud of her brave waking beast; a grand ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya returns to the lily pond hall and reaches up to wind the great carved pond carp where it hangs on its wires'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the great carved pond carp's flukes stir, its great copper body swinging slow, and a deep hum builds inside it like a song from far under the sea'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the great carved pond carp lets out a long, low, beautiful call that rolls through the blue hall, and turns gently on its wires as if swimming; when the children come they stand hushed beneath it, gazing up in wonder, and Priya feels she has given the museum its very heart back; a wondrous ending'>>

ending.title: the great carved pond carp Sounds

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya slips into the orchid dome and fits the seed-small winder to a painted garden moth with the lightest touch'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the foil wings quiver, unfold, and catch the the sky warming toward golden sunrise in a hundred colours as the little spring takes hold'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='a painted garden moth lifts from its glass fern and flutters in slow bright circles beneath the starry dome, and one by one Priya winds a few more until a little cloud of colour drifts through the conservatory; the children who come will chase them with shining eyes; a delicate, magical ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Priya goes to the once-drowsy garden deer that frightened her earlier and, no longer afraid, fits the gardener's dawn key to wind it true'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the once-drowsy garden deer steadies as the proper wind fills its spring, its lurching gone, its movements smooth and calm at last'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the once-drowsy garden deer that lurched so scarily in the dark now sways gently and lifts its trunk in a soft, friendly trumpet, tame and calm and no longer a runaway; the morning children ride giggling in a circle around it, and Priya is proudest of all to have made the frightening thing gentle; a heart-warming ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='old Marigold's note hinted at a deeper secret hidden in the museum, and Priya burns to know it; she can read old Marigold's private journal on the bench, follow where a second garden firefly, twin to Wisp keeps leading, or open the one locked cabinet in the shed in the workshop'>>

choices.label: Follow a second garden firefly, twin to Wisp.
Open the one locked cabinet in the shed.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='old Marigold's private journal is thick and worn, and Priya turns its pages by candle light, her firefly Wisp curled warm against her arm'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='page by page she learns how old Marigold built the whole menagerie himself, animal by animal, over a long and lonely lifetime of loving work'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the last entry tells of his dearest wish, that one day a child would come who cared enough to keep the animals alive after him'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='Priya closes old Marigold's private journal understanding everything now, that the menagerie was one old man's life poured lovingly into brass and tin, and that his greatest hope was simply a child like her; she leaves him a note promising to come back, and when old Marigold reads it he knows his life's work is safe; a tender, meaningful ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='a second garden firefly, twin to Wisp keeps darting ahead and looking back, plainly wanting Priya to follow; she can chase after it into the dark, or coax it gently to come to her instead'>>

choices.label: Chase the mouse into the dark.
Coax the mouse gently to you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='a second garden firefly, twin to Wisp leads Priya to a tiny door behind the skirting where a hidden nest of garden fireflies sleeps'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='a second garden firefly, twin to Wisp leads Priya to old Marigold's secret, a hidden nest of garden fireflies he made for company, and she winds them one by one until a dozen tiny helpers scurry out to tend the sleeping museum; old Marigold is overjoyed that someone found his small friends, and her firefly Wisp now has a whole family; a sweet, surprising ending'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='a second garden firefly, twin to Wisp will not be caught and simply sits, whiskers twitching, keeping Priya company through the last of the night; she never learns quite where it wanted to lead her, but she is not alone, and dawn finds the two of them waiting quietly together for the keepers to arrive; a calm, gentle ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the one locked cabinet in the shed is old and beautiful, and Priya is sure it holds old Marigold's greatest secret; she can try the tiny keys on the garden ring one by one, or force the little lock with a workshop tool'>>

choices.label: Try the tiny keys on the garden ring one by one.
Force the lock with a tool.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='patiently Priya tries key after key until at last one turns with a soft click, and the one locked cabinet in the shed's door swings open'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='inside stands a small worn clay songbird, older than all the rest, with a note that it must always be kept safe'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the one locked cabinet in the shed opens on old Marigold's first creation, a small worn clay songbird, the seed of the whole menagerie; Priya winds it with the greatest care and the old rabbit hops slowly, faithfully, as it must have a lifetime ago; old Marigold can hardly believe his first friend lives again, and calls Priya the finest thing that ever happened to his museum; a precious, moving ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='Priya forces the little lock with a tool, but the delicate old catch snaps and the one locked cabinet in the shed jams tight shut, refusing to open at all; nothing is harmed that a keeper cannot mend, but she never sees the secret inside, and must wait rather sheepishly to tell old Marigold she was too impatient; a small setback'>>

ending.title: the one locked cabinet in the shed Stays Shut

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='perhaps the bravest thing is simply to keep the sleeping museum safe and let the sky warming toward golden sunrise come; but even so Priya has choices, she can let the animals sleep and wait, wind just one favourite before the light, or wait by the door to meet whoever comes'>>

choices.label: Let them sleep and wait for morning.
Wait by the door to meet someone.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Priya settles on the root-clock chamber's floor with her firefly Wisp in her lap, watching the grey light climb the tall windows over the sleeping animals'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='Priya keeps the whole menagerie safe through the night and lets the sky warming toward golden sunrise come gently, the animals still and sleeping, everything exactly as the keeper left it; she did not fix the great spring, but nothing came to harm, and when the doors open she can tell the keepers all she learned; a safe, quiet, unfinished ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Priya thinks of all the animals and picks the one that touched her most, hurrying to it with the gardener's dawn key as the sky pales'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='she fits the gardener's dawn key with care and gives it just enough wind to wake for the morning, saving the rest for the keepers'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the animal stirs to gentle life under her hands, and she steps back to watch, glad she chose it'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='Priya wakes one single favourite animal to greet the sky warming toward golden sunrise while the rest sleep on, safe and ready; when the children come they find one wonderful moving creature among the still ones, a promise of all the wonders to come once old Marigold mends the rest, and Priya treasures the quiet magic of her one small waking; a lovely, hopeful ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='Priya waits by the great front door as the light grows, and she can watch for the museum keepers arriving, leave a helpful note pinned where they will see it, or tuck herself out of sight until she understands who is coming'>>

choices.label: Leave a helpful note for the morning.
Hide until you know who it is.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Priya writes out everything on a gardener's pad, the broken spring, a fresh coiled vine-spring, the steps to mend it, and pins it where the morning staff cannot miss it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='she props the gardener's dawn key beside it, ready for whoever comes, and waits nearby to explain if they need her'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='a gardener's pad tells the morning keepers exactly how to mend the great spring, and with it they wake the whole menagerie by opening time; the children never know how close the museum came to a silent day, but Priya knows, and the grateful keepers say her clear thinking saved the morning; a clever, satisfying ending'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='Priya waits quietly and meets the first keeper through the door at dawn, and tells her the whole story of the broken spring and the long night; the keeper listens amazed, thanks her kindly, and sees her safely home, and though Priya did not mend the menagerie herself, she leaves knowing she helped; a calm, friendly ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='Priya hides as the door opens, but it is only the early gardener's helper, who does not know a child is there and locks the inner gate on the way through, shutting Priya in the hall a while longer; she is safe and it is soon sorted out, but her chance to explain the broken spring slips by, and the menagerie sleeps through the morning; a gentle setback'>>

ending.title: Caught by the early gardener's helper
